then why did they come to watch the video?
all this was meant for is to provide music from a game. if someone doesnt like the music they shouldnt have come. in this case it is a rating of how well the video did what it was intended to do. if it was original music it would make sense to dislike it if you didnt enjoy it as the point of the video is to share the music and to get feedback from  those who choose to watch it

author: @Maliki321

text: That would be by design, unfortunately. Gameboy cartridges weren't equipped with a battery in those days (some of the later were, particularly Pokémon®). So, the Gameboy itself having no means whatsoever to memorise any data at all, resetting it via the on/off switch would indeed erase your score. I have Tetris 1.0 and 1.1 (no visual or gameplay difference whatsoever) and both have the same 'issue'
